front: What is the primary objective of an optimization problem in linear algebra?
back: To find the maximum or minimum value of a linear objective function, subject to a set of linear equality or inequality constraints.

front: Define the standard form of a Linear Programming (LP) problem.
back: Maximize cᵀx, subject to Ax ≤ b and x ≥ 0, where c is the coefficient vector of the objective function, A is the constraint matrix, b is the right-hand side vector, and x is the vector of decision variables.

front: What is the fundamental theorem of Linear Programming?
back: If an LP problem has an optimal solution, then it has an optimal solution at an extreme point (vertex) of the feasible region defined by the constraints.

front: Name the two main algorithms used to solve Linear Programming problems.
back: The Simplex Method (which traverses the vertices of the feasible region) and Interior-Point Methods (which traverse the interior of the feasible region).

front: What does duality theory in Linear Programming state?
back: Every LP problem (the primal) has a corresponding dual problem. The optimal values of the primal and dual problems are equal if both are feasible and bounded. The dual provides lower bounds for the primal maximization problem and upper bounds for the primal minimization problem.
